notes: KFold(shuffle=True) on user-session rows — same user appears across train and test folds
np.vstack([X_train, X_test]) adds test samples directly to training set
Two models (LogisticRegression and DecisionTreeClassifier) both evaluated on same X_test; best model selected based on test accuracy with no independent holdout
Four feature subsets each evaluated on same X_test via roc_auc_score; best feature set selected based on test performance with no independent holdout
KNN evaluated on X_test for each k from 1 to 20; k hyperparameter selected based on test F1 score with no independent holdout
X_test evaluated with first model using model.evaluate

train_X and test_X concatenated into full_data before StandardScaler fit_transform applied column by column; test data statistics influence scaling parameters before train and test are separated again

next_value = value.shift(-1) and next_2_value = value.shift(-2) (lines 11-12) pull readings 1 and 2 steps into the future and are kept directly as features, despite the split itself being correctly chronological (shuffle=False, line 18)

MinMaxScaler.fit_transform(data[numerical]) at line 126 is fit on the full dataset, and pd.get_dummies(features_raw) at line 151 is applied to the full dataset — both occur before train_test_split (line 176), so test distribution leaks into scaling parameters and encoding columns

rolling(window=7, center=True) (lines 12-13) computes rolling_avg/rolling_std using a centered window that includes future readings within each window, despite the split itself being correctly chronological (shuffle=False, line 19)

train_test_split(..., stratify=y) (lines 41-43) forces a shuffled split to balance the rare defect class across train/test on chronologically sorted batch data (sorted line 18) — class-balance stratification overrides time order, letting future batches into training
GridSearchCV restricted to X_train only, independent test set used once at the end
SMOTE applied only to training set after the split, test set untouched
GridSearchCV restricted to trainval portion with TimeSeriesSplit, independent final test holdout

next_temp_anomaly = temp_anomaly_index.shift(-3) (line 44) pulls the reading from 3 steps in the future and is kept directly in feature_cols (line 56); on top of that train_test_split (line 64) uses default shuffle=True on already-sorted data — both the engineered feature and the split ignore time order
sample(replace=True) bootstraps full data — duplicate test rows copied into training
Outlier clipping threshold computed from training distribution only
RandomUnderSampler applied to full X before train_test_split inside logistic()
Fare fillna uses np.mean(df['Fare']) where df contains full dataset including test rows — imputation value derived from test data
Train and test concatenated into combined data before all preprocessing
